title: nepeta citriodora
intro: Nepeta citriodora, also known as lemon catmint, is a perennial plant in the mint family, characterized by its distinctive lemon-scented leaves and spikes of purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of lemon catmint include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Lemon catmint can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
